title: shell completion --format: various improvements
body:
see individual commit messages for details
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Fixes: #14223
Fixes: #14222

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Improved handling for the --format option shell completion.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago PTAL

I am sure this will create a new diff with your script.

Nice. A whole lot of date fields now get a lot of subfields (like Day, Hour, Local, Minute, UTC, etc) which I'm just going to ignore for now. Three fields removed from `podman events` (.Recycle, .ToHumanReadable, .ToHumanString), all of which are ones that I added manually to the man page in my WIP PR. And the big change is in `podman pod inspect`, a whole bunch of new fields appearing under `.InfraConfig`:
```
.CPUPeriod
.CPUQuota
.CPUSetCPUs
.DNSOption
.DNSSearch
.DNSServer
.HostAdd
.HostNetwork
.NetworkOptions
.Networks
.NoManageHosts
.NoManageResolvConf
.PidNS
.PortBindings
.StaticIP
.StaticMAC
.UserNS
```

@edsantiago I am interested about https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14263/commits/b588e021fbb99eba490f7c780d47e1dc70bb874e, do you think it is a good idea? Would that help you as end user? In theory it would even be possible to get the arguments types so I could display that as well, but this would show the actual golang type names which will be meaningless to most people, i.e `time.Time`

Oh, that's interesting. My first reaction is that it doesn't give me much to go on. Is it possible to get arg names from the signature, and do `.Foo(bar, baz)`? I suspect not, and even if it is, I think we're getting into diminishing returns. Some of those might best be left documented in man pages, with no completion.

But I'll keep thinking about it. Thanks for the interesting idea.

> Oh, that's interesting. My first reaction is that it doesn't give me much to go on. Is it possible to get arg names from the signature, and do `.Foo(bar, baz)`? I suspect not, and even if it is, I think we're getting into diminishing returns. Some of those might best be left documented in man pages, with no completion.

No you can only get the actual data types, the variable names are not included in the binary. But even if I show types they could be pointless, for example I have no idea how the template logic would convert the args since we obviously can only set strings but many functions accept arbitrary args types. Unfortunately the go template documentation doesn't explain how it converts the args.

Anyhow... the `.InfraConfig` fields are easy to ignore (already done in my test branch); the three `podman events` fields are easy to take out (ditto); and, the crash reported in #14223 seems to be fixed. (You might want to add `Fixes: #14223` to your github description)

@edsantiago OK I believe #14222 should be fixed as well. I made a small change to also include the embedded struct name on the parent sine it is valid in the template use case, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14222#issuecomment-1127768096

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

Mostly bug fixes, however https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14263/commits/077a6c923cf0e26bb1b19ff2a2acbbad30d4582b is optional. I am interested if you think this is helpful? If not I can drop it from this PR.

Well, that sure changes things!

```
'podman volume ls --format --help' lists '.VolumeConfigResponse.UID'
```

Well that is the thing with these embedded structs. You can use them via two different paths but they are the same field.
This is why I did not want to show them by default since they are mostly redundant, only in some cases they are required (if there is a name conflict).
Restarted the failing tests.  I don't think they're flakes, but I wasn't 100% certain they were not.

LGTM but I really don't think the `this is a function and requires N arguments` clue is very helpful. I've tried to think of the appropriate target audience, and the only cases I can think of are someone who already knows what those time functions are (e.g., `.Add`, `.AddDate`) and knows how to plug in the magic arguments; or someone who _doesn't_ know what those time functions are, in which case `function with N arguments` is of little help. Especially since that someone, like me, would probably just see a field in the suggestions, and just try it to see what it does, and the error message gives me the same result:
```console
$ bin/podman ps --format '{{.Created.AddDate}}'
Error: template: ps:1:21: executing "ps" at <.Created.AddDate>: wrong number of args for AddDate: want 3 got 0
```
Anyhow, I'm fine with it either way but would like others in @containers/podman-maintainers to weigh in.

/lgtm
As far as @edsantiago comments, I think we could continue to discuss this in follow on PRs, if necessary.  Seems like a small amount of users would ever hit this.
